title: astragalus laricus
intro: Astragalus laricus, also known as Huang Qi, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of China and Tibet. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which help to boost the immune system and increase its ability to fight off diseases. The medicinal properties of Astragalus laricus include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-viral effects, making it beneficial for various health conditions, such as fatigue, anemia, and respiratory issues. Astragalus laricus can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
